varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana var. australis
varieties.origin: Native variety, South Carolina to Texas coastal plain, naturally occurring

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana 'Henry Hicks'
varieties.origin: USA, 1937. Selected by John Wister at Scott Arboretum, Pennsylvania. Named for nurseryman Henry Hicks (1870-1954)
varieties.availability: Not commonly available in Australian nurseries. Would require import or special cultivation.

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana 'Moonglow' (='Jim Wilson')
varieties.origin: USA, 1975. Selected by Earl Cully, Illinois, from var. australis seedlings
varieties.availability: Not available in Australian nurseries currently.

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana 'Northern Belle'
varieties.origin: USA, late 20th century (also known as 'Ned's Northern Belle')

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana 'Santa Rosa'
varieties.origin: USA, before 1990. Selected from Florida seed source by Bob McCartney, Woodlanders Nursery

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana 'Sweet Thing'
varieties.origin: USA, 1990s. Selected by George Dodson and FC Boyd III (='Perry Paige')

varieties.name: Magnolia grandiflora 'Little Gem'
varieties.origin: USA, compact cultivar selected for ornamental value
varieties.availability: READILY AVAILABLE at Sunshine Coast Plants, Brisbane nurseries. Common cultivar across Queensland.

varieties.name: Magnolia grandiflora 'Teddy Bear'
varieties.origin: USA, discovered by William Head, marketed as 'Southern Charm'
varieties.availability: READILY AVAILABLE. Sunshine Coast Plants, Oxley Nursery Brisbane, widely stocked across Queensland nurseries.

varieties.name: Magnolia figo 'Port Wine' (Michelia figo)
varieties.availability: READILY AVAILABLE. Nursery Near Me Australia, Sunshine Coast Garden Centre, widely available in Brisbane nurseries.

varieties.name: Magnolia champaca (Champaka, Chembakam) 🌱
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Indomalaya realm (South Asia, Southeast Asia, Southern China). Native throughout Western Ghats including Kerala. Sanskrit: चम्पक (campaka)
varieties.availability: Available in Kerala nurseries, temple gardens, traditional cultivation. Protected from logging in some states. Seeds and saplings obtainable locally.

varieties.name: Magnolia grandiflora (Southern Magnolia)
varieties.origin: Native to southeastern United States, widely cultivated globally in tropical-subtropical zones
varieties.availability: Available from select nurseries in Kerala, more common in hill station areas. Cultivated in botanical gardens. Not as common as native species.

varieties.name: Magnolia champaca 'Nok Noi' (Little Bird Champaca)
varieties.origin: Hybrid cultivar, naturally dwarf form. Developed by Top Tropicals 2021, trademarked (PP)
varieties.availability: Very rare, collectible variety. Limited availability, under development by Top Tropicals. Expected release 2026+. Not yet available in India.

varieties.name: Magnolia maudiae (Michelia maudiae)
varieties.origin: Native to wide area of southern China (Hong Kong to Guizhou), widely cultivated in Chinese tropical-subtropical zones
varieties.availability: Not commonly available in Kerala. May be obtainable through specialty tropical plant nurseries or botanical garden exchanges.

varieties.name: Magnolia platypetala (Michelia platypetala)
varieties.origin: Native to Southeast Asia tropical regions
varieties.availability: Not commonly available in Kerala. Specialty import from Southeast Asian sources possible.

varieties.name: Magnolia figo (Banana Shrub)
varieties.origin: Native to China, cultivated for centuries
varieties.availability: May be available from specialty nurseries in Kerala, more common in cooler hill regions.
